description: Welcome message + message delivery after tenant ACTIVE

userStories.title: Send welcome Slack DM when tenant becomes ACTIVE and deliver subsequent messages to container
userStories.description: As a Slack user, I want to receive a welcome message when my workspace is ready, and have my subsequent messages forwarded to the agent and responses sent back to me.
acceptanceCriteria: After health poll marks tenant ACTIVE, control plane (or relay) sends a Slack DM to the user: "Your workspace is ready! You can start chatting now." using the SLACK_BOT_TOKEN via Slack chat.postMessage API
The welcome message is sent to the correct Slack channel (the DM channel from the original event)
Subsequent messages from the same user are forwarded to the tenant container via POST http://<container-ip>:3100/message with x-relay-token header
The container IP is resolved via docker inspect (not container name) since the host cannot resolve Docker container names
When the container message-server returns a response, the relay sends it back to the user via Slack chat.postMessage
The message_queue row is updated to status=DELIVERED after successful forwarding
The Slack channel_id from the original event must be stored in the message_queue or tenant record so the response can be sent to the correct DM
userStories.notes: The SLACK_BOT_TOKEN is available in the relay env vars. The relay already has the Slack SDK or can use fetch to call chat.postMessage. The container message-server.js uses openclaw agent --local --agent main --message TEXT --json --timeout 120. Store the slack_channel_id on the tenant record or message_queue so responses can be routed back.
